name: Speedy Cash
description_short: Speedy Cash offers payday loans up to $425, lines of credit up to $4,000, and title loans up to $4,000 with in-store application and same-day service at Memphis locations.
description_long:
Speedy Cash operates a network of storefront lending locations, with multiple branches across the Memphis, Tennessee area including the Summer Avenue location. The company specializes in short-term emergency lending products designed to bridge immediate cash needs between paychecks or during unexpected expenses. Their business model centers on rapid approval and funding, with physical store locations where customers can apply in person and receive personalized guidance through the lending process. The company has been operating under the "Tomorrow can be payday®" brand positioning for emergency cash solutions.

Speedy Cash offers three primary lending products: Payday Loans (up to $425 with repayment due on next payday), Lines of Credit (up to $4,000 with flexible draw-down and repayment over time), and Title Loans (up to $4,000 secured by vehicle collateral). Beyond lending, they provide money services including check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and Green Dot Visa debit card services. The Memphis Summer Avenue location operates with extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays, closing at 4pm Saturday and all day Sunday), supporting accessibility for working customers.

Speedy Cash distinguishes itself through in-person service with trained staff who can explain loan options, answer questions during application, and complete funding in a single store visit. They provide transparent pricing where borrowers know exact costs before signing. The availability of multiple loan types (installment, payday, title, and lines of credit) allows customers to select products matching their specific financial situations. The company also offers online application initiation and Spanish-language customer service (1-855-734-0111), expanding accessibility across language barriers.

However, Speedy Cash operates in the high-cost lending space with products that carry substantial interest and fees typical of payday and title lenders. While faster than traditional bank loans, these products are significantly more expensive than credit union alternatives or employer advances. The payday loan maximum of $425 is relatively modest, and title loans require vehicle collateral, limiting access for those without clear titles or suitable vehicles. Customers considering these products should exhaust alternatives like credit union PALs or employer advances, which offer lower costs under 36% APR.
pros: Payday loans available up to $425 with transparent pricing disclosed before signing
Lines of credit up to $4,000 with flexible draw-down matching variable expenses
Title loans up to $4,000 for customers with vehicle collateral
Extended weekday hours (9am-7pm Mon/Fri) accommodating working customers
In-store staff assistance explaining loan options and answering questions during application
Multiple money services (check cashing, wire transfers, money orders) in same location
Bilingual customer service available in English and Spanish
Online application initiation option to start process before in-store visit
cons: High-cost lending with interest rates and fees substantially higher than credit union alternatives or payday loan alternatives (PALs)
Title loans require clear vehicle title and proof of insurance, excluding customers without qualifying collateral
Payday loan maximum of $425 is modest for larger emergency expenses
Repayment structure (lump-sum on next payday) may create debt cycle for borrowers with tight budgets
No information provided about APR, finance charges, or total cost of borrowing on website
best_for: Employed workers needing $100-$400 in emergency cash before next paycheck with ability to repay lump-sum
Vehicle owners with clear title needing $1,000-$4,000 in collateralized loans over time
Customers needing flexible credit lines for fluctuating expenses up to $4,000
Individuals preferring in-person application with face-to-face staff guidance over online-only lending
diagnosis: Speedy Cash is best for employed individuals with immediate emergency cash needs ($100-$4,000) who can repay within weeks or months and prefer in-person service. Critical caveat: These are high-cost loans significantly more expensive than credit union PALs or employer advances—borrowers should exhaust alternatives offering APR under 36% before using payday or title loans, as the debt cycle risk is substantial.
services: Green Dot Visa debit card services
Vehicle appraisal for title loan qualification
